memory:memory/2026-05-01.md:1:25.snippet: # 2026-05-01 ## AgenToDo UI/UX Review — First Full Cycle ### What Happened - Previous session did full screenshot review, wrote findings to `reviews/agentodo-review-177DA06F.md`, then hung before filing issues - After restart: filed 10 issues (#234–243, #248), responded to comments, closed invalid ones - 14 issues fixed by the team already (fast turnaround) - 9 issues still open ### Lessons Learned (from Oleksandr's feedback) 1. **Always attach screenshots** — multiple issues got "attach screenshot" comments. No excuses. 2. **Verify which app you're looking at** — filed #241 about Claude's sidebar thinking it was AgenToDo. Embarrassing. 3. **Know standard UI patterns** — #218 was just a c

memory:memory/2026-05-01.md:36:57.snippet: - **The product IS the experience.** Don't file "the color is slightly off" — file "this misleads the user because..." - **Root cause > symptom.** Always dig into the code. Attach the file + line number. - **One issue = one clear fix.** If the fix isn't obvious from the report, rewrite it. - **Would this ship at Apple?** That's the bar. Not "is this technically broken" but "would a user feel delighted?" - **Simplify, don't add.** Default to removing clutter, not adding features. The calendar banner doesn't need a dismiss button — it needs to not exist in the main view. - **Cross-platform = one product.** If iOS and macOS show different numbers for the same data, the product is broken. Period

memory:memory/2026-05-01.md:18:39.snippet: 7. **File issues as you go** — don't batch. Session can hang, context can be lost. ### Rules for Future Reviews - Crop and attach a screenshot to EVERY issue - File each issue immediately upon discovery - Triple-check which window/app is in frame - Default stance: "should this exist?" before "this is missing" - Know platform conventions (time indicators, standard controls) before flagging - When in doubt, propose simplification over addition ### Screenshot Workflow for Issues 1. Capture relevant screenshot with `screencapture -o -x <path>` 2. Crop to highlight the bug area with `sips --cropToHeightWidth H W --cropOffset Y X` 3. Copy to `/tmp/agentodo-screenshots/` and `git add . && git com

Basic stuff. 4. **Don't assume setup context** — #234, onboarding wasn't bugged, data was pre-loaded intentionally. 5. **"Different" ≠ "wrong"** — #243 pagination style was intentional design. Read the design language before applying HIG dogmatically. 6. **Question the premise first** — #229 was filed as "missing subtitle" when the right take was "subtitles are clutter, remove them." Think like Jobs: simplify, don't add. 7. **File issues as you go** — don't batch. Session can hang, con

memory:memory/2026-05-02.md:35:49.snippet: **Swift 6 + XCTest is broken:** The UI test base class uses `@MainActor` but `XCTestCase.setUpWithError()` is `nonisolated` in Swift 6. Every `XCTContext.runActivity` closure also triggers sendability errors. Fix: set UI test target to `SWIFT_VERSION: 5.0` + `SWIFT_STRICT_CONCURRENCY: minimal` in project.yml. **Signing cert expired:** "Apple Development: Monro AI (F38256T2W2)" was invalid. Oleksandr fixed it mid-session. Ad-hoc signing (`CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY=-`) works for building but NOT for running UI tests (entitlements require real signing). **Location features are architecturally ambitious but half-baked:** - CLMonitor crashes on macOS with WhenInUse auth (needs Always) - Cross-device d

memory:memory/2026-05-02.md:46:54.snippet: **Attachment system has a marker problem:** Notes store raw `{{att:UUID:storedName}}` markers that are only rendered by the MarkdownEditor. Any other context (CLI, widget, export, clipboard) shows the raw markers. No fallback rendering. ### Lessons Learned (New) 1. **Run the existing tests first.** The app had a full XCUITest suite that was failing to compile AND had 76% failure rate. Running these tests early would have identified the background-thread issue much sooner. 2. **Code review catches more than screenshots.** On a Mac mini without a display/mouse, deep code review found issues I never would have caught visually — threading violations, race conditions, dedup logic bugs, missing v

memory:memory/2026-05-02.md:53:64.snippet: 5. **File size limits are easy to forget.** The attachment system has no size cap. Same for OmniFocus imports (no cancel). Always ask: "what if the input is 100x larger than expected?" 6. **Batch filing with subagents is efficient.** Filing 20 issues per subagent, with pre-written descriptions, takes ~3 minutes per batch. Much faster than filing one at a time. 7. **The signing certificate expires/revokes periodically.** Always check `security find-identity -v -p codesigning` before starting a test session that needs real signing. ### Architecture Notes - **Build system:** XcodeGen → xcodebuild. Must run `xcodegen generate` after project.yml changes. - **DEVELOPER_DIR** must point to `/Appli
